mechanism_narrative: A sustained low federal funds rate indicates accommodative monetary policy, reducing discount rates and boosting equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: High unemployment rates signal weak labor market conditions, reducing consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, which predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite high unemployment signals.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production and housing starts indicates robust economic activity, boosting corporate profits and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for the interaction term drops below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts signal weakening demand for construction, predicting negative S&P 500 returns during late-cycle expansion periods due to reduced economic momentum.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where housing starts decline during late-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation pressures (CPI) signal potential monetary tightening, increasing discount rates and reducing equity valuations, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where CPI rises above 0.5% monthly.

mechanism_narrative: Strong consumer spending (RSAFS) indicates resilient demand, driving corporate earnings growth and predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for RSAFS drops below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) signals a tightening labor market, increasing wage pressures and corporate costs, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where payroll growth exceeds 100k monthly.

mechanism_narrative: Weak industrial production combined with rising consumer demand (INDPRO and RSAFS) indicates supply-demand imbalances, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction term between INDPRO and RSAFS shows Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
